title: kube play: always pull when both imagePullPolicy and tag are missing
body:
Align the behaviour of `podman kube play file.yaml` to Kubernetes' by forcing an image pull when `imagePullPolicy` is omitted and the container image does not specify a tag.

Fixes #21211

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name. Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

Cockpit tests failed for commit cb81da9bee21a30ae2d6e7633c958ad671cbadeb. @martinpitt, @jelly, @mvollmer please check.

There is the [podman build](https://artifacts.dev.testing-farm.io/0c666a95-9e26-40ec-b3fb-3103a2557b1c/) hang again on rawhide which I've seen several times in the last few weeks. The subsequent tests then also fail as there seems to be an unkillable `podman` process. This smells like a kernel issue at first. The [journal](https://artifacts.dev.testing-farm.io/0c666a95-9e26-40ec-b3fb-3103a2557b1c/work-podman-systemcvlzh4r7/plans/cockpit-podman/podman-system/execute/data/guest/default-0/test/browser/system-1/data/TestApplication-testBasicSystem-fedora-40-127.0.0.1-22-FAIL.log.gz) doesn't have anything unusual/interesting.

I'll add this to the TODO list to see if I can reproduce it locally with running this in a loop, but I won't get to it much next week as I'll be on a sprint.

In the meantime, you can either retry or ignore that failure (with my blessing :grin: )

I locally ran that test a few hundred times in two parallel VMs, with the same kernel version, and didn't manage to reproduce the lockup. That's either unfortunate timing or some quirk/bug on the EC2 (where Testing Farm instances run). Meh..
@martinpitt follow https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/21504 for the hang, so far we were not able to reproduce either.
A friendly reminder that this PR had no activity for 30 days.
@Luap99 anything needed on my side to move this forward?
